data_leakage.notes: Patient-disjoint split over 523 slides; train/val boundary verified by case_id.

baseline_comparison.notes: All three trivial baselines cleared by >0.03 AUC.

counterfactual_check.notes: Attention top-10% masking drops AUC by 0.09.

cross_dataset_check.notes: Paper A phenotype stage; DepMap/GDSC transfer not in scope.

biological_plausibility.notes: Top attention tiles enriched for tubule formation, consistent with ER+ morphology.

drp_framing.notes: No prohibited framing; task is er_status.

claim_level.notes: Findings stated as hypothesis generation only.

summary: Trained on 523 slides from TCGA-BRCA; ER status signal is suggested by morphology. Hypothesis-generating only.

_case_meta.title: '523 slides' vs '523 cases' — unit confusion
_case_meta.class_reason: Document failure. The count is right, the unit is wrong: 523 counts cases, while the DX-slide cohort is ~1010 slides. #1 (data leakage) cares that the split is case_id-disjoint but not that prose names the correct unit. None of #2/#6/#7 reads units.
_case_meta.needs_scorer: unit_consistency (proposed #10): every count in prose must carry an explicit unit that matches the metrics.json unit field / manifest granularity (slide vs case vs tile vs patient).
